provenance.source_head: eeb83563 (parent of the S2 commit; the commit adding this file is the SHA_E engine freeze)
provenance.note: Columns EXTRACTED from DECISIONS.md D17 + addenda + micro-decisions, BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §8 (sharp edges), basalt_eval_protocol.md, and qa/evidence-bundles/basalt/phase2/BASALT_G2_SUMMARY.md §6/§9. Enforced by tests/invariant_matrix_binding.rs (§2.4 mechanism: include_str! file binding + test-fn name assertion + fixture tripping — see DECISIONS.md D17 micro-decisions §2.4). Human-readable twin: BASALT_RECON.md §13. HONEST CELLS ONLY: cells record what a test actually proves today, not what a phase promises.

columns.invariant: An unknown or lane-uncovered tensor type produces a NAMED, typed refusal — never a silent fall-through, never a panic.
sources: BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §3.4 (Unknown(i32) fail-closed baseline; §8 Unknown ordering/serde edge)
RUNNABLE_LANE_SPEC.md principle #2 via src/runnable/admit.rs module doc (machine-readable refusals)
BASALT_G2_SUMMARY.md §6 (refusal-point move: parse-level Unknown(40) refusal became the lane refusals)

columns.invariant: A NaN-sentinel UE4M3 scale byte (raw 0x7F or 0xFF) in an NVFP4 file refuses at load; decode itself stays pin-CPU-bitwise (0x7F flushes to 0.0, 0xFF decodes 240.0) per D17 addendum. Zero scales admit.
sources: DECISIONS.md D17 T5 + G1 addendum (pin backends disagree on 0xFF; admission refuses both sentinels)
BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §3.2/§8 (NaN scale bytes -> load error, fail closed; the zero-scale half of §3.2 was superseded by signed T5: zero scales admit — see BASALT_RECON.md §13 reconciliation note)

columns.invariant: D-B2: an NVFP4 GGUF carrying `.scale`/`.input_scale` sidecar tensors (ModelOpt convention) refuses whole-file — silently ignoring sidecar scales would compute wrong logits.
sources: DECISIONS.md D17 D-B2 + addendum 2 (enforced in BOTH lanes)
BASALT_G2_SUMMARY.md §6 (admission wiring) / §7 (wild-file: coverage stays synthetic-fixture)

columns.invariant: Per-tensor scale applied exactly once, never dropped or doubled. v1 posture (pin truth): NO out-of-block per-tensor scale exists on the wire — refusal of sidecar-bearing files IS the exactly-once enforcement; the four in-block UE4M3 sub-scales' single application is pinned bit-exact by the golden decode fixtures.
sources: BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §3.3 + §8 (dropped/doubled scale = the campaign's most likely functional bug)
DECISIONS.md D17 D-B2 (reframed by pin truth: in-block sub-scales only; fail closed on sidecars)

columns.invariant: NVFP4 wire-length divisibility: element counts not divisible by the 64-element superblock refuse — never a silent pad. (Conductor §8 states K%16 as quantizer keep-list guidance; the consume-side wire unit is the 64-element superblock, so enforcement is %64 — reconciled in BASALT_RECON.md §13.)
sources: BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §8 (K%16 != 0: keep source precision, never pad silently)
DECISIONS.md D17 D-B1 (64-element/36-byte superblock is the wire unit)

columns.invariant: D-B3 boundary: everything OUTSIDE the gemma4+NVFP4 pilot carve-out keeps its pre-BASALT behavior — gemma4 files without NVFP4 and all other architectures keep their refusals byte-for-byte; formats a lane already served keep loading.
sources: DECISIONS.md D17 addendum 2 (architecture-axis carve-out, signed at G2)
BASALT_G2_SUMMARY.md §6/§9 (refusal-move receipts; review fix: generic refusal message byte-identical to pre-BASALT main)

columns.invariant: Warm/cached serve state must key on (or be structurally unable to survive) a change of quant identity — an NVFP4 load must never reuse Q8-era cached state (the PR #419 warm-hit-bypass lesson).
sources: BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §8 (prompt-prefix cache / cached-state must key on tensor type/quant identity; PR #419 lesson verbatim)

columns.invariant: Amendment 3 §9: NVFP4 admission refuses off-Windows with the named TK2 error ('NVFP4 is Windows-only in this release; see SUPPORT_MATRIX') — a runtime gate inside ordinary code, not a #[cfg] wall; fires AFTER the D-B3/D-B2 checks so signed postures stay platform-independent.
sources: DECISIONS.md D17 micro-decisions §9.1

lanes.name: Runnable lane (admission, dequant, smoke)
sources: src/runnable/admit.rs
src/runnable/dequant.rs
src/runnable/smoke.rs

lanes.name: Gemma4 wire lane (CPU; the NVFP4-executing lane in this release)
sources: src/gemma4_runtime.rs
src/inference.rs
src/tensor/mod.rs (decode_nvfp4_tensor, nvfp4_find_nan_scale)

lanes.name: CUDA-resident lane (Gemma4CudaResident; NVFP4 residency is Phase 4)
sources: src/cuda_resident.rs
src/gemma4_runtime.rs (Gemma4CudaResident, nvfp4_cuda_lane_check)

lanes.name: Metal lane (Gemma4GpuRuntime, macOS; NVFP4 never wired per §9.3)
sources: src/gemma4_runtime.rs (Gemma4GpuRuntime::load, nvfp4_metal_lane_check)

lanes.name: Native quantizer (Phase 2b — never scheduled; D-B5 pin-tool-only)

I-unknown-type.note: Defense in depth: the file boundary refuses FIRST at parse (src/gguf/reader.rs tensor_nbytes -> 'unknown or removed GGML type', tripped by the fixture), so admission's uncovered-type reject is the metadata-level backstop for types that parse but have no dequant.

I-nan-scale.note: Seam split is deliberate and documented in src/runnable/dequant.rs: admission is metadata-only (never sees wire bytes), so the sentinel refusal fires in decode_nvfp4_tensor via runnable::dequant. The fixture trip drives decode_nvfp4_tensor — this lane's consumer — on the fixture's real on-disk wire bytes.

I-sidecar.note: The S2 fixture carries tokenizer.ggml.model so admission's fixed axis order reaches the quant axis and the D-B2 reject fires end-to-end from file bytes on every platform (the sidecar check precedes the §9 platform gate by design).

I-scale-once.fixture: seam:v1 posture (D-B2, pin truth) — no out-of-block per-tensor scale exists on the wire, so there is no apply-twice/apply-zero path to trip from a file; refusal of sidecar-bearing files IS the exactly-once enforcement (see I-sidecar fixture), and the in-block sub-scales' single application is pinned bit-exact by the golden decode fixtures
I-scale-once.note: If a future phase lands sidecar application, this cell must be re-signed with dropped/doubled failure-mode tests per BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §3.3.

I-k-div.note: The file boundary refuses at parse (src/gguf/reader.rs tensor_nbytes: first dim not divisible by block size 64); decode_nvfp4_tensor's %64 and byte-length refusals are the in-lane backstop for non-file callers.

I-carveout.note: The three admit tests pin the boundary in all directions (non-gemma4+NVFP4 refuses with the D-B3 scope message; gemma4-without-NVFP4 keeps the architecture refusal; the generic uncovered-quant message is byte-identical to pre-BASALT main per the S1 review fix). The smoke test pins admit-then-fail exclusion: the pilot stays oracle-refused until G3.

I-cache-quant.reason: Structural — no warm state exists on this lane to key: the runnable serve bridge routes only qwen35/gemma3 (src/api/mod.rs fn is_runnable_serve_arch), so a gemma4+NVFP4 pilot file never gets a RunnableServeRuntime; RunnableServeRuntime generation is &self-immutable with no cross-request decode cache (src/api/mod.rs struct RunnableServeRuntime); runnable smoke is one-shot and stays oracle-refused for the pilot until G3 (src/runnable/smoke.rs); the Llama-path prompt-prefix cache is unreachable from this lane (its payload is a LlamaInferenceSession). The meta-test asserts these structural anchors still exist in the bound sources.

I-plat.note: cfg-twinned per §9.1: the refusal leg runs on the ubuntu/macos CI legs; the Windows leg proves the same fixture ADMITS (positive control that the gate does not misfire where NVFP4 is supported, and the first file-boundary admission proof of the D-B3 carve-out).

I-unknown-type.note: This lane's file boundary is read_metadata (Gemma4Runtime::load's first act), so the parse-level unknown-type refusal tripped by the fixture guards it; WireQuant::new's typed UnsupportedTensorType arm is the in-lane refusal for types that parse but are not wire-covered (driven directly in the named test — a tiny full file cannot reach WireQuant, config parsing fails first). SHA_E3 correction (same honesty pattern as the L3 SHA_E review correction at commit 0e2f7f95): the cell previously covered only load-time type admission, and the column's 'never a panic' clause was NOT true forward of load — a latent pre-BASALT gap let lane-COVERED K-quant types (Q4_K/Q6_K) load as LAYER-PROJECTION weights and hit matvec_q's K-quant unreachable! at forward time (the per-layer/batched projection call sites bypassed the top-level matvec's family routing; first reachable via the campaign's Q4K-mm/Q4_K_M rows, probe-proven by the S3 legs). Fixed under the Amendment 3 §3 freeze-move as a crash fix: projections now dispatch per activation family (Q8_K for K-quants, mirroring top-level matvec), Q5_K — gather-only in this lane — refuses TYPED at load in every matvec role (require_matvec_capable), and the Q4_1 gather arm became a typed error; the companion tests pin the routing bit-equal to the pre-existing correct route and the two typed refusals.

I-nan-scale.fixture: seam:full-file trip not reachable — LlamaModelConfig::from_gguf runs before any WireQuant::new sentinel scan and fails first on a tiny fixture's missing gemma4 config KVs; tests/nvfp4_wire_lane_refusals.rs::nan_sentinel_fixture_trips_the_scan_seam drives the SAME nvfp4_find_nan_scale seam on nvfp4_nan_sentinel_trip.gguf's real on-disk wire bytes and ASSERTS the non-reachability (S1 precedent)
I-nan-scale.note: Zero scales admit (T5 boundary), asserted in the same test. Decode stays pin-CPU-bitwise per D17 addendum (0xFF -> 240.0), pinned by the golden decode-table suite.

I-sidecar.note: END-TO-END through Gemma4Runtime::load — nvfp4_sidecar_check fires immediately after read_metadata, before config parsing and before the §9 platform gate, so the trip proves the D-B2 posture on every platform.

I-scale-once.fixture: seam:v1 posture (D-B2, pin truth) — no out-of-block per-tensor scale exists on the wire; the sidecar refusal (see I-sidecar, end-to-end fixture) IS the exactly-once enforcement, and the in-block sub-scales' single application through matvec/matmul/row-dot is bitwise-pinned against the decode paths and the pin-extracted E4B spot-check fixture
I-scale-once.note: The P4 CUDA path must re-sign this cell at its own seam (kernel epilogue vs host-side row scale — BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §5 Phase 4).

I-k-div.note: HONEST scope: the parse boundary (tensor_nbytes, first dim % 64) is this lane's enforcement for files — WireQuant::new's own block-alignment and byte-size re-checks are defense-in-depth that no GGUF-parsed NVFP4 tensor can reach (parse guarantees first-dim divisibility), so they carry no lane-native NVFP4 tripping test.

I-carveout.reason: Structural — D-B3's carve-out is a runnable-admission architecture-axis construct; this lane has no architecture axis to carve: Gemma4Runtime::load serves gemma4 only by construction (LlamaModelConfig::from_gguf must yield a gemma4 config or load fails with 'not a gemma4 model', src/gemma4_runtime.rs), NVFP4 joined the wire-covered set outright, and outside-the-set refusal continuity is this lane's I-unknown-type cell. Sanctioned-row byte-identity after the S1 closure is CERT-receipted (qa/evidence-bundles/basalt/phase3/row_rehash_post_closure.txt), not lane-testable.

I-cache-quant.reason: Structural — cached state cannot survive onto an NVFP4 load: (1) NVFP4 executes only via Gemma4ServeRuntime, resolved per model id from AppState::gemma4_runtimes (src/api/mod.rs fn resolve_gemma4_runtime_for_model) — entries are replaced wholesale on reload and cleared on unload; a gemma4 model without its runtime gets a typed model_not_ready 503, never a silent fall-through to the Llama path; (2) the Llama prompt-prefix cache's payload is a LlamaInferenceSession with ZERO call sites in gemma4_runtime.rs (fn lookup_prompt_prefix_cache / fn store_prompt_prefix_cache live in src/api/mod.rs and are reached only by the Llama handlers); (3) that cache's key already includes model_id AND model_path — distinct quant rows are distinct files (G2 §1 receipts) — and the single slot is cleared on every active-model switch and on unload (fn clear_prompt_prefix_cache call sites in load/unload). Key-miss on model change is additionally pinned by the Llama-lane test prompt_prefix_cache_reuses_exact_prompt_and_invalidates_key_changes (src/api/mod.rs). The meta-test asserts these structural anchors still exist in the bound source.

I-plat.note: cfg-twinned per §9.1. File-boundary coverage rides the S1 NaN fixture's load leg: on non-Windows, Gemma4Runtime::load on that fixture is asserted to fail with the named TK2 message (the §9 gate fires before config parsing there), executed on the ubuntu/macos CI legs.

I-unknown-type.fixture: seam:Gemma4CudaResident::load requires a bindable real gemma4 model plus a CUDA device — not constructible under the <4KB synthetic-fixture rule; nvfp4_cuda_lane_check is cfg-independent and driven directly on every WireFormat outside the lane's covered set
I-unknown-type.note: Phase 4 (G4) lift: the lane's covered set is now Q8_0/Q4_0/Q4_1/NVFP4 (nvfp4_gemv shipped), so the check ADMITS NVFP4 (positive control cuda_lane_check_admits_nvfp4_after_the_phase4_lift, inverting the pre-Phase-4 refusal — ratchet R3) and still refuses EVERY remaining uncovered format typed and named — the K-quants the CPU wire lane serves (Q4K/Q5K/Q6K, incl. the campaign's own Q4K-mm and Q4_K_M rows, which would otherwise reach the from_wire repack panic under CAMELID_GEMMA4_CUDA=1).

I-nan-scale.fixture: seam:the load-time sentinel REFUSAL is inherited — Gemma4CudaResident::load's first act is Gemma4Runtime::load, whose WireQuant::new nvfp4_find_nan_scale runs before any CUDA work; a CUDA-bound full-file trip needs a real gemma4 model + a CUDA device (not constructible under the <4KB fixture rule), so the S1 fixture trips the shared scan seam and the lane-native test proves the below-the-seam DECODE semantics on the kernel itself
I-nan-scale.note: P4 earns this lane-natively two ways: (1) the inherited load-time refusal (companion wire_quant_new_refuses_nan_sentinel_scale_bytes drives the same WireQuant seam the CUDA entry shares; the S1 fixture trip is the on-disk leg); (2) the NEW kernel test feeds crafted 0x7F/0xFF scale blocks through nvfp4_gemv BELOW the refusal seam and bit-matches nvfp4_wire_row_dot, proving pin-CPU-bitwise decode (0x7F->0.0, 0xFF->240.0 — deliberately NOT the pin CUDA-intrinsic double-flush; D17 addendum/T5).

I-sidecar.fixture: seam:Gemma4CudaResident::load's first act is Gemma4Runtime::load, whose nvfp4_sidecar_check (D-B2) fires before any CUDA work — so the CUDA lane cannot bind a sidecar-bearing NVFP4 file; a CUDA-bound full-file trip is not constructible under the <4KB fixture rule, so the shared helper is unit-driven here and the end-to-end file trip on the same seam is the L2 cell's nvfp4_sidecar_trip.gguf
I-sidecar.note: The lane-native test drives the exact shared guard the CUDA entry inherits (sidecar refuses D-B2, pilot admits), and the end-to-end companion trips Gemma4Runtime::load — the CUDA lane's first act — on the on-disk sidecar fixture. No sidecar application exists on the v1 wire (I-scale-once), so this refusal IS the enforcement.

I-scale-once.fixture: seam:v1 posture (D-B2, pin truth) — the P4 D-B2 seam (BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §5 Phase 4: kernel epilogue vs host-side row scale) resolves as NEITHER EXISTS: there is no out-of-block per-tensor scale on the wire, and the four in-block UE4M3 sub-scales are applied exactly once inside the per-sub-block term (nvfp4_gemv), the same place the CPU oracle applies them
I-scale-once.note: Bit-parity on random + crafted blocks (nvfp4_gemv_matches_oracle, 100% bit-identical vs nvfp4_wire_row_dot) is the dropped/doubled-sub-scale killer — a scale applied zero or twice cannot survive same-bytes bit-comparison across the shapes; the sidecar-refusal companion enforces the whole-file half. If a future phase lands sidecar application, re-sign per BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §3.3.

I-k-div.note: The file-parse boundary (tensor_nbytes, first dim % 64) still refuses non-%64 NVFP4 tensors before load (fixture trip). The P4 kernel earns its OWN divisibility proof lane-natively: launch_nvfp4_gemv refuses an odd Q8_0-block count typed (Nvfp4LaunchError::OddBlocksPerRow — one 64-value superblock spans two 32-value activation blocks), and nvfp4_gemv_matches_oracle exercises the 64-element indexing at 1 and many superblocks (n_sb 1/2/40/160).

I-carveout.note: Boundary-preservation direction: the K-quant refusal must not bleed onto the formats this lane serves — Q8_0/Q4_0/Q4_1 (pre-BASALT) AND NVFP4 (Phase 4) projections, plus the no-projection case, keep loading. Post-Phase-4 the covered set grew by NVFP4 only; every K-quant stays typed-refused (I-unknown-type).

I-cache-quant.reason: Structural — NVFP4 residency adds NO new cross-instance warm state: the resident KV caches, the captured decode CUDA graph (SendGraph), the cached_tokens prefix, and the gpu_head buffers are all per-Gemma4CudaResident fields; instances are keyed by model id in AppState::gemma4_runtimes (src/api/mod.rs fn resolve_gemma4_runtime_for_model), replaced wholesale on reload and cleared on unload, and distinct quant rows are distinct files (G2 §1). The P4 NVFP4 upload path is upw raw-wire passthrough into fresh per-instance CudaSlices — it introduces no new cache to key. The Llama prompt-prefix cache (LlamaInferenceSession payload) has zero call sites in gemma4_runtime.rs, asserted by the meta-test's na-anchor check. Per orchestrator decision (recon §5 Q3) this closes na-with-structural-reasons; no behavioral reload test is pre-committed.

I-plat.fixture: seam:Gemma4CudaResident::load requires a real gemma4 model + a CUDA device — not constructible under the <4KB fixture rule; the §9 gate (nvfp4_windows_only_check) fires inside Gemma4Runtime::load, the CUDA lane's FIRST act, BEFORE any CUDA initialization, so the cfg-twin observes the refusal with no GPU, and the shared load-leg file trip is the L2 cell's nvfp4_nan_sentinel_trip.gguf
I-plat.note: cfg-twinned per §9.1 (its own named L3 test, not an inheritance note): off-Windows legs assert the CUDA lane's shared entry gate yields the named TK2 refusal before any CUDA init; the Windows leg asserts the pilot shape admits so the CUDA lane can bind (D-B3). The companion off-Windows refusal + the S1 fixture's load leg (Gemma4Runtime::load, the CUDA entry's first act) are the file-boundary coverage.

I-unknown-type.fixture: seam:Gemma4GpuRuntime::load is cfg(target_os = "macos") and requires a real model; nvfp4_metal_lane_check is cfg-independent and unit-driven on every host, including a deep (non-layer-0) NVFP4 tensor that the lane's layer-0-only format gate would otherwise mis-bind
I-unknown-type.note: UPGRADE over the prescribed na (§9.3): S1 wired a typed refusal naming 'BASALT L4: na' into the Metal load path — stronger than prescribed, recorded honestly as enforced.

I-nan-scale.reason: NVFP4 never binds on the Metal lane: nvfp4_metal_lane_check refuses ANY NVFP4 tensor at Gemma4GpuRuntime::load (src/gemma4_runtime.rs), before any wire-byte scan territory — there is no NVFP4 decode on this lane to guard (§9.3 posture; the refusal itself is this lane's I-unknown-type/I-plat cells).

I-sidecar.fixture: seam:the Metal load path is cfg(target_os = "macos") + real-model-bound; the shared nvfp4_sidecar_check helper (called at Gemma4GpuRuntime::load before nvfp4_metal_lane_check) is unit-driven on every host, and its end-to-end file trip is the L2 cell's nvfp4_sidecar_trip.gguf
I-sidecar.note: UPGRADE over the prescribed na: S1's review fix routed the D-B2 sidecar check through the Metal load path too (it previously never ran there), so a sidecar-bearing file cannot slip past the signed posture via macOS.

I-scale-once.reason: No NVFP4 execution exists on this lane to apply a scale once or twice — nvfp4_metal_lane_check refuses at load (src/gemma4_runtime.rs, Gemma4GpuRuntime::load); the sidecar half of the v1 posture is this lane's I-sidecar cell (enforced upgrade).

I-k-div.reason: No NVFP4 wire is ever consumed on this lane (refused at load by nvfp4_metal_lane_check, src/gemma4_runtime.rs); file-boundary divisibility is guarded lane-independently at parse (src/gguf/reader.rs tensor_nbytes, tripped by nvfp4_k_div_trip.gguf).

I-carveout.note: Boundary-preservation direction: files without NVFP4 keep loading on the Metal lane exactly as pre-BASALT — the new refusal touches only NVFP4-bearing files.

I-cache-quant.reason: No NVFP4 warm state can exist on this lane: the load-time refusal (nvfp4_metal_lane_check, src/gemma4_runtime.rs) precedes any session/KV construction, so no cached state with NVFP4 provenance is ever created to be mis-reused.

I-plat.note: The lane-local refusal IS the platform posture here (Metal = macOS, NVFP4 is Windows-only): the same typed refusal enforces both this cell and I-unknown-type — recorded in both deliberately. The macOS CPU wire path additionally gets the §9 TK2 refusal (companion test runs on the macos CI leg).

I-unknown-type.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): no native quantizer lane exists; the in-tree encode_nvfp4_block is TEST-ANCHORING ONLY (src/tensor/mod.rs) and is not an admission/load surface.

I-nan-scale.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): no native quantizer emits scale bytes; the test-anchoring encoder's outputs are golden-pinned against the pin's quantize_row_nvfp4_ref, which never emits sentinel scales for finite input.

I-sidecar.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): no Camelid tool writes NVFP4 GGUFs, so no producer-side sidecar policy exists to enforce; consume-side D-B2 is L1/L2's cells.

I-scale-once.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): no producer-side scale computation exists; the pin's llama-quantize is the sole producer (deterministic, receipted at G2).

I-k-div.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): keep-list/divisibility decisions belong to the pin tool's per-tensor override path (the G2 receipts pin exactly which 294 tensors were converted).

I-carveout.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): no lane surface exists on which a D-B3 boundary could be preserved or violated.

I-cache-quant.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): no serve/warm state of any kind.

I-plat.reason: Phase 2b never scheduled — D-B5 pin-tool-only (DECISIONS.md D17): no admission surface for the §9 gate to guard.

rules.rule: Lane-adding PRs add a ROW: any PR that gives NVFP4 a new execution or admission surface (a new backend lane, a serve bridge, a quantizer) must add its lane to `lanes` and fill all 8 cells honestly in the same PR — the meta-test's full-population assertion makes a missing cell a test failure.

Invariant-signing PRs add a COLUMN: any PR that signs a new NVFP4 invariant (a DECISIONS.md D17 addendum, a new conductor sharp edge with refusal semantics) must add its column to `columns` (with the source cited) and fill it for all lanes in the same PR.

Gate G4 closes open:P4 — the P4 (CUDA-resident NVFP4) PR must flip every open:P4 cell to enforced (with lane-native tests) or na (with structural reasons); the meta-test rejects open cells whose phase has landed, so merging P4 without closing them fails CI.
Scheduling Phase 2b (native quantizer) re-opens the L5 row: its na cells must be re-signed as enforced/open in the P2b PR, same mechanics as R3.
